title: When people think about healthcare careers, nursing often comes to mind first—but there are many ways to make a differen...
content_text:
When people think about healthcare careers, nursing often comes to mind first—but there are many ways to make a difference in healthcare.
From fast-paced hospitals to small private offices, healthcare careers offer a wide variety of work environments and opportunities.
This summer, explore healthcare career paths at ChattState info sessions for:
🩺 EMS
💙 Nursing (RN)
💊 Pharmacy Tech
🦷 Dental Hygiene
🐾 Veterinary Tech
☢️ Nuclear Medicine
Learn about program requirements, career opportunities, and how you can build a future helping others.
Get more info on all Nursing and Allied Health programs at https://ow.ly/JclG50Z2bJ2
original_source: Job & Training Resources Social Media Events

title: Do you believe every child deserves to learn how to read—and that well-supported tutors can help make that possible? Chattanooga 2...
content_text: Do you believe every child deserves to learn how to read—and that well-supported tutors can help make that possible? Chattanooga 2.0 is #hiring a Program Manager for an AmeriCorps-funded early literacy tutoring program that places trained tutors in some of Hamilton County’s highest-need elementary schools. The AmeriCorps program manager will support AmeriCorps volunteers, coordinate across schools, track implementation closely, and help ensure our students receive consistent, high-quality reading support every day. For more information and how to apply, visit: https://lnkd.in/ehCsQJ4Y #AmeriCorps #VolunteerTennessee This project is funded under a Grant Contract with the State of Tennessee, Volunteer Tennessee.

title: From the classroom to the trails, UTC students are helping care for Chattanooga’s outdoor spaces. 🌲🥾 Students in UTC’s...
content_text:
From the classroom to the trails, UTC students are helping care for Chattanooga’s outdoor spaces. 🌲🥾
Students in UTC’s new Trail Building and Management course are gaining hands-on experience by clearing trails, removing hazards and helping restore access to local recreation areas throughout the community.
The course combines outdoor recreation, conservation and real-world workforce training while helping meet a growing need for trail management and stewardship in Chattanooga.
Read more: https://blog.utc.edu/…/the-chattanooga-trails-utc-program-…/

title: Turns out play really is for adults too! Our YPC Members Only night at the Creative Discovery Museum was such a fun one. Between e...
content_text: Turns out play really is for adults too! Our YPC Members Only night at the Creative Discovery Museum was such a fun one. Between exploring the interactive exhibits, reconnecting with familiar faces, meeting new people, and getting surprisingly competitive over Networking Bingo, there was something happening in every corner of the museum. Members also got to tap into their creative side with string art woodworking projects — creating custom designs with yarn, nails, and wood to take home at the end of the night. And shoutout to Binh and Rogelio for taking home the Networking Bingo wins and their $50 gift cards courtesy of Frazier Five & Dime. Thanks for helping make the night even more fun. Thanks to everyone who came out and made this members-only event such a great time!

title: DJ Khaled voice: “Anotha one.” 📢 We’re excited to share another opportunity with the Southeast Tennessee Area Agency on Aging & Di...
content_text: DJ Khaled voice: “Anotha one.” 📢 We’re excited to share another opportunity with the Southeast Tennessee Area Agency on Aging & Disability. We’re hiring a Quality Assurance Coordinator to support state and federally funded programs across our 10-county service area. This role is ideal for someone who is organized, analytical, proactive, and passionate about supporting quality services for older adults and individuals with disabilities. Responsibilities include provider monitoring, site visits, technical assistance, satisfaction survey coordination, reporting, and supporting continuous quality improvement initiatives. Qualifications: ✔ Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university OR equivalent work-related experience ✔ Strong written and verbal communication skills ✔ Ability to maintain confidentiality ✔ Ability to work independently and collaboratively 📍 Position serves Southeast Tennessee’s 10-county region 📅 Applications must be submitted by Friday, June 5, 2026 To apply, please send your resume and cover letter to: Don Kellerman at dkellerman@sedev.org Or mail to: ATTN: Human Resources P.O. Box 4757 Chattanooga, TN 37405 The Southeast Tennessee Development District is an equal opportunity employer/program. Auxiliary aids and services are available upon request to individuals with disabilities. Tennessee Relay 711.

title: “I identify with Goodwill’s guiding principle that says, ‘Every time we touch a donation, something good should happen,’...
content_text:
“I identify with Goodwill’s guiding principle that says, ‘Every time we touch a donation, something good should happen,’” said Katie, Goodwill Learning and Development Training Coordinator. For Katie, this “something good” is reflected not only in her own career growth, but in the success of the Goodwill team members she now trains.
Before working at Goodwill, Katie earned a degree in animal science and worked in several industries, including food services. After she and her family moved from Kentucky to the Chattanooga area, she stayed at home to care for her two children for several years. When she decided to return to the workforce, she faced a familiar challenge that many caregivers encounter: a gap in employment and uncertainty about where to begin.
“My mom is a store associate at a Goodwill in Kentucky, and she told me about all the wonderful opportunities there,” said Katie. “I thought since it is such a great thing, maybe I could be a part of it too.”
Katie applied for a position at the Goodwill store and donation center in Fort Oglethorpe, Georgia. Recognizing her potential, Christy, the Director of Retail Operations, offered her a shift lead position with clear opportunities for growth.
Within three months, Katie was promoted to Assistant Manager. After three more months, she was promoted to Store Manager at our new, much larger Fort Oglethorpe store.
While enjoying store leadership, Katie found herself drawn back to a skill she discovered earlier in her career, training others.
“When I worked in food services, I realized how much I enjoyed teaching and helping people grow. When I saw the Goodwill Learning and Development Training Coordinator job posting, it was truly an ‘aha’ moment.”
Katie now serves as our Learning and Development Training Coordinator and says the role feels like a perfect fit. She shared, “I’ve found my passion again.”
When you donate or shop at Goodwill, you’re doing more than decluttering or finding a great deal; you’re investing in people like Katie. Your support fuels career pathways and leadership development right here in our community. Our mission is to help people transform potential into opportunity, and our vision is a community where everyone can thrive. It’s more than Goodwill; it’s good work. #GoodwillChatt
original_source: Job & Training Resources Social Media Events

Partnerships like these create real impact in our community helping women gain confidence, resources, professional development, and opportunities for a brighter future. Thank you to the Chattanooga Rotary Club for believing in the mission and investing in women who are ready to thrive. ✨ #DressForSuccess #SuccessStartsNow #Chattanooga #CommunityImpact

title: I enjoyed attending the Women’s Leadership Collaborative event today, which brought together more than 190 women. The CEO of EPB,...
content_text: I enjoyed attending the Women’s Leadership Collaborative event today, which brought together more than 190 women. The CEO of EPB, Janet Rehberg, PMP, CEM , spoke about emotional intelligence and leadership, sharing several key takeaways: - Surround yourself with men and women who will advocate for you when you are not present. - Communication is one of the most impactful skills one can possess. - Networking is not just about who you know, but about who knows you. Engage in networking with intentionality. Thank you to Janet and everyone involved in organizing this amazing event.

title: 📣 We’re Hiring! 📣 The Southeast Tennessee Development District Area Agency on Aging & Disability is seeking a Home Delivered Meals...
content_text: 📣 We’re Hiring! 📣 The Southeast Tennessee Development District Area Agency on Aging & Disability is seeking a Home Delivered Meals Site Coordinator to serve the Monteagle and Tracy City areas. This position plays an important role in supporting older adults in our communities by coordinating and overseeing home delivered meal services. Responsibilities include managing daily meal operations, maintaining accurate reporting, ensuring compliance with program guidelines, and working closely with volunteers and community partners. 🕘 Hours: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM We’re looking for someone who is: ✔ Organized and dependable ✔ A strong communicator ✔ Able to work independently and manage multiple tasks ✔ Passionate about serving older adults and the community Experience working with older adults or in a community-based setting is preferred. 📅 Application Deadline: Friday, May 15, 2026 📧 Send resumes, cover letter, and references to: Crystal Scogin at cscogin@sedev.org 📬 Or mail to: Southeast Tennessee Development District P.O. Box 4757 Chattanooga, TN 37405 ATTN: Human Resources 📞 Questions? Call 423-424-4279 The Southeast Tennessee Development District is an equal opportunity employer/program. content_text: The Heavy Equipment Operator's Course FALL COHORT is Registering NOW! Chattanooga State’s Heavy Equipment Operator Course is preparing individuals with the skills employers are actively seeking—and the results speak for themselves. Several Chattanooga-area construction companies are already offering job possibilities to students upon successful completion of the program. title: Strong economies are built by skilled workers. During National Apprenticeship Week, we celebrate the power of Registered Apprentic...
content_text: Strong economies are built by skilled workers. During National Apprenticeship Week, we celebrate the power of Registered Apprenticeships to create real career pathways through hands-on learning and real-world experience. These “earn-as-you-learn” opportunities allow individuals to build valuable skills while contributing to the industries that keep our communities moving forward. From construction and advanced manufacturing to healthcare and technology, apprenticeships are helping strengthen our workforce and open doors to high-quality careers. Learn more at https://lnkd.in/d2u4HNZE #NationalApprenticeshipWeek #WorkforceDevelopment #CareerPathways

Whether you're starting fresh or looking to advance your path, this flexible online program lets you learn on your schedule—without putting your life on hold.

title: When you step into a supportive space like the Clubhouse, you’re given opportunities to learn more about yourself and fi...
content_text:
When you step into a supportive space like the Clubhouse, you’re given opportunities to learn more about yourself and find your purpose, just like Jerry has. 🧡
Jerry, “The Mayor of the AIM Center,” earned his title from a staff member who saw his daily dedication and strong work ethic, and it stuck because of how deeply connected he is to the community. A member since 1994, Jerry started at the original McCallie Avenue location in the dish room, where he began bussing dishes with fellow member, Bud. After his first tour of the Clubhouse, he recalls, “I thought it was unique…this would be a good fit for me.”
Today, he leads The Jerry Short Show on AIM TV, highlighting community events and keeping members informed, and he enjoys working in the café, especially serving as chef of the day.
Outside the Clubhouse, Jerry works concessions at Erlanger Park for the Chattanooga Lookouts, now in his sixth season, and previously worked at the Chattanooga Times Free Press. At AIM Center, he helps interview new staff, builds strong friendships, and even previously served on the Board of Directors. “I love taking ownership of the Clubhouse. It gives me a sense of pride,” he says.
Stories like Jerry’s are possible because of a community that believes in recovery, purpose, and opportunity. During our AIM Center Day of Giving, you can help ensure that members like Jerry continue to find connection, build skills, and take on meaningful roles both inside and outside the Clubhouse.
Your support fuels programs that empower members to grow, lead, and thrive, just like Jerry has for over 30 years. AIM Center Day of Giving is quickly approaching on May 6th, but you can make a difference now and give ahead of the big day by clicking here ➡ onecau.se/_h58xg1
